title: 10 medicinal constituents of Marrubium peregrinum (deadnettle)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Marrubium peregrinum lamalbidiol is a bioactive compound extracted from its leaves. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal disorders. Lamalbidiol exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective against microbial infections and reducing inflammation. Its chemical structure also shows potential as an antioxidant agent, offering protection against oxidative stress and cellular damage.

Marrubium peregrinum marubenine is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. Its leaves are lance-shaped with toothed margins, while its flowers are white or pinkish in color, forming dense spikes on the stem. Marubenine has been used traditionally to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties and anti-inflammatory compounds.

Marrubium peregrinum rosmarinic acid is a bioactive compound extracted from the plant's leaves. This phenolic acid has been extensively studied for its numerous biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Rosmarinic acid has been shown to exhibit significant activity against various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, making it a promising natural preservative in food and pharmaceutical industries.

Marrubium peregrinum oleic acid is a naturally occurring fatty acid found in its leaves and stems. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, fever, and respiratory problems. Oleic acid in Marrubium peregrinum has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential therapeutic agent for several diseases. Further research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and uses.

Marrubium peregrinum ursolic acid is a bioactive compound derived from its leaves and stems. It has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Ursolic acid has also shown promising results in preclinical studies against various diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disorders, and neurodegenerative diseases. Its unique chemical structure makes it an attractive target for further research and potential therapeutic applications.

Marrubium peregrinum gallic acid is a phenolic compound found in its leaves and stems. It has been extensively studied for its potential medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Gallic acid has been shown to exhibit inhibitory effects on the growth of certain cancer cell lines, making it a promising candidate for future research into the treatment of various diseases. Its unique structure and biological activity make it an attractive target for further investigation.

Marrubium peregrinum quercetin is a flavonoid compound found in its leaves. This bioactive molecule has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Quercetin has been shown to exhibit potent inhibitory effects on pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, making it a promising natural therapy for various diseases such as cardiovascular disorders, cancer, and respiratory tract infections. Its unique molecular structure also enables quercetin to cross the blood-brain barrier, allowing it to potentially influence brain function and cognition.

Marrubium peregrinum kaempferol is a flavonoid compound that has been isolated from its leaves and flowers. It exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to the plant's traditional use in treating various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. Further research on kaempferol's biological activities and potential health benefits is ongoing, with preliminary findings suggesting its promise in combating oxidative stress and inflammation-related diseases.

Marrubium peregrinum isorhapontigenin is a bioactive compound isolated from the aerial parts of Marrubium peregrinum, a plant species native to Europe and Asia. It has been shown to possess various pharmacological activities, including antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory effects. Isorhapontigenin has also been reported to exhibit cytotoxicity against certain cancer cell lines, suggesting its potential as a natural chemotherapeutic agent.

Marrubium peregrinum betulinic acid is a naturally occurring compound isolated from the plant. It has been found to exhibit various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Betulinic acid has been studied for its potential therapeutic applications, particularly in cancer treatment, where it has shown promising results in inhibiting tumor growth and inducing apoptosis in cancer cells.

intro: Marrubium peregrinum has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. These compounds contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The constituents in deadnettle have been shown to reduce pain and swelling, making it a potential natural remedy for arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, the plant's antioxidants may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, potentially improving overall health and well-being.
intro_desc: Marrubium peregrinum has active constituents such as iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and essential oils. These constituents possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The iridoid glycosides, specifically marrubiin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. This can improve people's lives by providing relief from gastrointestinal issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and promoting overall gut health.
